qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable single-player Pong with solid controls and scoring to 7, but visuals are only approximate to classic Pong and the CPU/serve flow make balance and polish feel somewhat off.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: An exceptionally polished Pong implementation featuring smooth CPU AI, sound effects, score tracking, and a complete game loop.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Solid Pong implementation with proper paddle/ball physics, CPU AI, score tracking to 7, pause, serve timer, and sound effects; minor issues include the ball being launched at startup before IDLE mode and the center line color being too dim compared to classic Pong.

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable single-player Pong with working scoring, AI, pause, and restart, but visuals are only loosely authentic and there are minor logic/code quality issues such as odd paddle drawing code and update-after-render ordering.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: An exceptionally faithful and polished Pong implementation featuring smooth AI, sound effects, and a complete game loop with high-score tracking.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Core Pong mechanics work with paddle movement, ball physics, scoring to 7, and CPU AI, but the drawPaddle function has a nonsensical ternary expression that may cause rendering issues, the paddle x-coordinates don't clearly account for paddle width centering, and the CPU AI's low speed cap (300 vs ball speed 350+) makes it beatable but the proportional tracking with smoothing factor 0.08 may feel sluggish.
